note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The CME front appears quite deflected from the source, triangulating well around E95-E100 despite the observed eruption source in SDO AIA imagery at S09E72.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

Triangulation between 2 frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and 1 frame of STEREO A COR2 due to data gaps beginning at 2025-12-27T17:36Z and 2025-12-27T17:38Z respectively. CME front is deflected slightly northward, not perfectly fitting the swpc_cat lemniscate, but tracked as well as possible for this analysis.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. STEREO A COR2 was not available for this measurement.
Measurement based on source location. This measurement uses limited available SOHO LASCO C2 imagery due to the CME's faintness.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint front of this CME with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No clear source has been found to confirm this measurement.

One-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1) analysis based on the likely (but rather faint) source: eruption W of AR 14317. The CME front is not seen in STEREO A COR2, so this analysis cannot be corroborated by triangulation.
Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement based on the source longitude. A triangulated measurement was not available due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Measurement based on the source longitude. A triangulated measurement was not available due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint dimpled front with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No coronal signatures of a far-sided source seen in EUV imagery.

Analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and including the northern portion of this fuzzy, dimpled front. Takes into account later timeframes of coronagraphs. Possible source is the C class flare from Active Region 14324.

A very speculative analysis based on the fit of fronts in two coronagraphs (relatively clear front in STEREO A COR2 and a super faint partial halo front in SOHO LASCO C2 coronagraph). Not confirmed by a clear source, but there is an impulsive flare from Active Region 14325 at the time that fits the bill.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. CME half-width less than 10 degrees.

Single spacecraft 3D analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 as STEREO A COR2 imagery not available for this CME, using the flare source of S17E42 as the longitude. As such, the longitude could easily vary E30-E50 if deflection occurred.

Single spacecraft 3D analysis with SOHO LASCO C2 as STEREO A COR2 imagery not available for this CME, using the flare source of S17E42 as the base longitude and adjusting for the shock width and shape, ending up around longitude E22.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.
SHOCK measurement based best fit of SHOCK feature using running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Best-fit measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and on the visible location of the filament. There is also a wider, more northern portion that is not visible by the time the CME makes it into SOHO LASCO C3. Because the extra feature is not visible later in SOHO LASCO C3, the bright bulk has been prioritized for the measurement. The extra, wider feature is more prominent in GOES CCOR-1, but it is still not as bright as the main bulk. The approximate longitude of the filament footpoints is around E60 to E80. Since a triangulated measurement cannot be made as there is no STEREO A COR2 imagery for this event, a starting point of E60 was used for this measurement. Because of this, there will inherently be a greater than normal degree of uncertainty associated with this measurement.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source is behind the NW limb in SDO and this event is covered by an unusually long data gap in STEREO A imagery.

Very approximate analysis of the faint wide shock of this CME with one-coronagraph method (SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs), based on the fit of the partial halo front with coronagraphs. The source is far-sided and the exact longitude is not known.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO and STEREO A for triangulation of the longitude.
Low confidence measurement as the front of the CME is not easily seen in available coronagraph imagery.
Triangulation of the CME leading edge to determine longitude.
Leading edge measurement using all available spacecraft out to 12 solar radii which suggests strong southern deflection.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A SECCHI COR2A, and GOES CCOR-1 out to about 12 solar radii.
Low confidence leading edge measurement using triangulation between GOES and STEREO A to determine the originating source longitude.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location. Only one frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery was available for this measurement, so the fit is approximate.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as the visible on-disk source location.
Measurement based on the source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
This measurement is somewhat subjective because this CME is only visible in STEREO A COR2. The longitude in the measurement is based on the source location on the Earth-facing disk.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source on the Earth-facing disk.
Measurement based on the best f it between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement following the wider feature seen in SOHO LASCO imagery. The measured parameters were obtained between the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Bulk measurement tracking the inner, brighter feature based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
SHOCK measurement following the fainter, outer feature. The measured parameters were obtained from the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
One-coronagraph analysis (SOHO LASCO) based on the location of the source eruption.

Analysis of the fainter leading shock front with one coronagraph method (SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1) based on the shape of the front; this analysis fits the location of the source - flare and eruption near AR 14334.
Approximate, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis of the southern lobe of this two-lobed event based on the location of the source. The source is a long filament, so analysis is very approximate.
Approximate, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis of the eastern lobe of this complex eruption.

HIGHLY SPECULATIVE ANALYSIS. Very faint front measured to the N/NW starting with the potential source location and adjusting to fit the front. Deflection highly likely with this event based on dimming seen on solar disk.
Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

STEREO A COR2 only measurement due to SOHO data gap and GOES CCOR-1 pylon obstruction of the CME, using the observed source centered around S60E20 as a basis. As such the latitude and longitude could easily vary +-5 degrees either direction, but high confidence in being out of the ecliptic.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Very speculative analysis since the only possible (and extremely minor) source seems to be on/close/behind the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 but (a very uncertain) fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and one or two rather questionable timestamps of STEREO A COR2) produces a fit of ~70 deg of longitude.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. SOHO LASCO C2 was not used as the CME moves more slowly in early frames.

Half width less than 10 degrees. Single spacecraft 3D analysis due to lack of COR2A imagery and CCOR-1 pylon obstruction, using the observed flare source S16W04 as a basis. However, observed deflection of the CME in SDO AIA imagery and the inability to triangulate with other coronagraphs makes this analysis speculative, and the latitude/longitude could easily range within +-10 degrees.

Single spacecraft 3D analysis due to lack of COR2A imagery and CCOR-1 pylon obstruction, using the observed dimming source S27W02 as a basis. However, observed deflection of the CME in SDO AIA imagery and the inability to triangulate with other coronagraphs makes this analysis speculative, and the latitude/longitude/half-width could easily range within +-5 degrees.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the potential source location. Limited imagery was used for this measurement due to the CME's faintness.
Measurement based on potential source location using GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Shock measurement based on the potential source location, with adjustment to best fit the shock of the CME in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.

Single spacecraft analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, using E115 as the source longitude as this CME is likely outflow from CME: 2026-01-11T22:45Z with E115 as its bulk. Longitude could easily vary +-10 degrees.

Single spacecraft analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, using E115 as the source longitude as this CME is likely outflow from CME: 2026-01-11T22:45Z with E115 as its bulk. Longitude could easily vary +-10 degrees.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

Best fit based on the possible SHOCK of the CME (fainter front ahead of bulk material) using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Longitude is estimated based on potential source location and best fit in available coronagraph imagery. Due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints, the parameters may be off by +/- 10 degrees.
Parameters based on best fit of potential BULK of CME. Tracking the brighter portion of the CME in available SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 difference imagery.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as a possible source location.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). There are two possible sources on disk, however neither of these sources closely corresponds to the fit.

Final but somewhat approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by the location of the likely source (C-class flare in AR 14340 (N15E05)). The CME front is very fuzzy in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph, as most of its propagation is covered by a large daily data gap in STEREO A. The CME is also partially obscured by the pylon in STEREO LASCO C3.
Approximate analysis based on the fit of two main lobes of this complex-shaped CME in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).
Approximate analysis of this far-sided CME made with the fit in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO). The source (filament eruption) is behind the NE limb.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Very approximate analysis of an extremely fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2). There seems to be eastern deflection of over 10 degrees, as the likely source eruption has the longitude of ~40 degrees.

Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is a small coronal hole to the east of the eruption location which may have caused deflection of the CME within the field of view.

Very speculative, approximate analysis based on the fit of this faint, fuzzy front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). The source is likely far-sided and no coronal signatures have been found in EUV imagery.

Very approximate analysis of an extremely fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2). There seems to be eastern deflection of over 10 degrees, as the likely source eruption has the longitude of ~40 degrees.

Very approximate analysis of an extremely fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2). There seems to be eastern deflection of over 10 degrees, as the likely source eruption has the longitude of ~40 degrees.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Approximate analysis of the wide faint shock front based on the fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) in the absence of SOHO LASCO imagery that is expected to be available later.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and on the location of the flare, made once SOHO LASCO imagery became available. Analysis is complicated by the CME having two overlapping lobes, one more to the SE and another a bit more central.
Measurement based on the approximate source longitude.
Measurement based on the source longitude.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the potential source location using GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 only, as the CME front is better observed in later frames.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by the location of the likely source - a minor filament eruption centered (N30E01).
Very approximate 3D analysis based on the fit of the CME front with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) in the absence of SOHO LASCO imagery. The source is far-sided.
Approximate analysis based on the fit of the front with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the location of the likely source (minor filament eruption).
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery with relation to the suspected source. Parameters may vary by +/- 10 degrees due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The parameters may vary due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints.

Approximate analysis of this far-sided faint event based on the fit with three coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found in the EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.

Analysis based on the fit of the narrow front with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3). Very well corresponds to the likely source - an eruption from (N15E10), with significant northern deflection, likely due to a large coronal hole being just to the south of the eruption site.

A very speculative, approximate measurement due to the faintness of the front in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph. This analysis is based on the fit of the front in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source (moving field lines indicating a far-sided filament eruption) is either on or (more likely) behind the E limb.
Approximate analysis of a faint dimpled front based on its fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).

Very approximate analysis of this far-sided CME, based on the fitting of the fronts seen in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). Fitting is difficult because the CME consists of several lobes.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and a single frame from STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the source location. As this event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery, a triangulated measurement was not available to confirm possible deflection from the source. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

A rather speculative, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis based on the possible source - a faint and slowly developing eruption seen in the NE of the solar disk in SDO AIA 193. The CME is fully covered by the daily gap in STEREO A COR2, so we are unable to confirm this analysis with triangulation.

Approximate, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis, based on the location of the source - flare and eruption in/around AR 14345. The CME is fully covered by the daily gap in STEREO A COR2, so we are unable to confirm this analysis with triangulation.

Somewhat speculative one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis, based on the location of the source - flare and eruption from AR 14342. The CME is fully covered by the daily gap in STEREO A COR2, so we are unable to confirm this analysis with triangulation.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters account for northern deflection from a nearby coronal hole.
Parameters based on best fit using the approximate source location and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. This fit suggests a high Southern deflection.
Measurement based on potential source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Limited imagery was available for this measurement due to the faintness of the leading edge of the CME.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on speculative source around AR 14359 (N20E30).
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Updated, triangulated measurement based on later frames including STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source location near the NE limb.
Analysis based on the location of the source (C6.0 flare and eruption in the former Active Region 14351, seen in STEREO A EUV imagery after 2026-01-31T04:50Z.
Very approximate, speculative analysis based on the fit with 3 coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found in EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.

Very approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the location of the possible source, with some minor deflection westwards from the source eruption.

3D single spacecraft analysis with SOHO LASCO C3 and one LASCO C2 frame, using the observed source around W141.8 in STEREO A EUVI imagery as the basis, and the longitude could easily  vary +-10 degrees. Analysis is quite speculative due to LASCO data quality with an ongoing campaign, STEREO A COR2 data gap, and LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 fronts not aligning well with the lemniscate tool.

Single spacecraft 3D analysis with limited SOHO LASCO C3 frames due to an ongoing campaign, also due to poor LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 image quality and availability. Uses the flare source at N16E56 and dimming source at N23E58 finding some northward deflection, although some variance in the latitude and longitude is expected with the single-spacecraft analysis.

Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This measurement includes a very faint wider front visible in SOHO LASCO C2 frames. The brightest portion of this CME is visible along the southern edge of the CME with a much more narrow appearance. This CME in STEREO A is more clearly defined in difference imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Final, approximate and speculative analysis based on the fitting with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) once STEREO A COR2 imagery became available after the end of the daily data gap in STEREO A. This analysis leaves out the earliest, more northward, faint front seen in SOHO C2 and CCOR-1 as the likely corresponding very faint front in the first couple of available STEREO A COR2 after the data gap is very unclear/obscured by brighter following front. The CME is treated as one event in this analysis. The fitting indicates a likely significant deflection westwards from the source location (location of the X-class flares from Active Region 14366), possibly due to the effect of the coronal hole to the NE of the Active Region location. Analysis is complicated by the asymmetrical shape of the CME in STEREO A COR2, the fact that the start of the CME is obscured by the data gap in STEREO and the difficulty separating multiple overlapping fronts in SOHO and CCOR-1.

Speculative analysis based on the fit of the front in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). It is not clear which of the multiple fronts in SOHO/CCOR-1 corresponds to the more clear front in STEREO A. The possible source (flare from AR 14366) is more eastward than the longitude we are obtaining from triangulation, so there might be westward deflection of the CME.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There may be some uncertainty with this fit due to the faintness of the CME.
There is a high degree of uncertainty in this measurement due to a speculative source and a lack of available triangulation.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

SOHO LASCO C2 only 3D measurement using the eruptive source at N22W65, finding a good fit with minimal deflection, although some variation in the latitude/longitude is possible without triangulation here. LASCO C3's pylon obscures the faint front in running difference imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

BULK triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and three available STEREO A COR2 frames before its data gap. Multiple concentric fronts are observed in LASCO C2, treated as one front measuring on the leading most edge and capturing the full width of the brighter material.

SHOCK front analysis, of the very faint shock feature observed in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and even more faintly in STEREO A COR2. This feature is trackable enough in running difference imagery to get a triangulation, but difficult to properly discern the width, so this analysis is presented with some speculation but a good 3D fit between coronagraphs.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This fit suggests a SW deflection. Feature appears wider in SOHO LASCO than it does in STEREO A COR2.
Updated measurement using a few frames of STEREO Ahead.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Very approximate, speculative analysis based on an approximate fit of the very faint, fuzzy, uncertain front in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the location of the minor source eruption seen mostly in STEREO A EUVI 195 as minor dimming. There seems to be a northern deflection from the source location.
Analysis based solely on the fit of the faint, fuzzy front in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No clear source has been found in EUV imagery.
Measurement based on the potential source location in NSO GONG data.
Measurement of the wider, fainter shock feature.
Measurement based on source location using available SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on potential source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. Limited available imagery was used, as the CME's leading edge fades away quickly.
Measurement based on potential source location on the east limb. Due to the uncertainty in the source eruption location, the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location. This feature appears to exhibit a strong Northern deflection.

Parameters based on potential source location area and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. This is specifically measuring the most narrow portion of the feature visible in difference imagery. There is a much slower, slightly wider portion that may also be associated with this source that measured with speeds below 150 km/s. The longitude of this CME may vary by +/- 10 degrees due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints of the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Measurement based on the approximate source location. As this event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs, a triangulated measurement was not available for this event. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on visible source location near the NW limb (W90).
Parameters based on the middle range between potential source longitudes (80 to 100 degrees) using available SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Longitude may vary by +/- 10 degrees.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Measurement based on source location and the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement with additional imagery based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measured longitude based on the approximate source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. There is some degree of uncertainty in this measurement, and longitudes ranging from W77 to W90 were measured.

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge is very faint and difficult to track in SOHO LASCO C2, so only SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 were used for the measurement.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit of the SHOCK feature in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
SOHO C2/C3 measurement based on longitude of previous CME: 2026-02-16T04:24Z.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). No source has been found for this CME, confirming its far-sidedness.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit of the leading edge in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The CME is best visible in STEREO A COR2.
Preliminary analysis based on best fit between limited frames from SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the approximate location of the source eruption, as seen in STEREO A EUV 195/304 imagery and on the fit of this narrow CME in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO coronagraphs.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. No source candidate has been found for this CME.

Measurement based on best fit of the leading edge in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. There is some uncertainty in these parameters as longitudes ranging from ~W115 to ~W131 yield reasonable measurements.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Approximate analysis based on the fit of this front in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).
Approximate analysis based on the fit of this narrow front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2). No clear source has been found in the EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery during this event, a stereoscopic measurement was not available. The measured parameters may therefore vary as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Approximate analysis based on the later timestamps of GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3 coronagraphs and fitting the early timestamps of STEREO A COR2. The CME is seen speeding up in later timestamps.

Approximate analysis with one-coronagraph (GOES CCOR-1) method, based on the location of the source eruption. The CME is not seen in SOHO LASCO coronagraph because of the scheduled data gap that has not yet backfilled and in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph due to the night time data gap.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.
Preliminary approximate one-coronagraph (STEREO A COR2) analysis based on the location of the likely source (C2.6 flare and eruption). Awaiting for backfill of SOHO LASCO data to triangulate.

Analysis based on the fit in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). If the suggested faint filament eruption is the source of this CME, there is a significant deflection northward.
Speculative analysis based on the fit of a very minor front in STEREO A coronagraph with the more clear front in SOHO LASCO. No source found for this CME to confirm this analysis.

Analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1); shows minor deflections eastwards (which is likely due to the coronal hole to the west of the erupting filament).
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge is too fuzzy to consistently track once it reaches the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Parameters based on best fit using available STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the potential source location. Due to uncertainty in the source location, this longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.
Measurement based on potential source location.
Measurement based on the potential source location.
Very approximate one coronagraph (GOES CCOR-1) analysis based on the approximate location of AR 14384 (N11E90), which is rotating on the visible disk. The longitude can differ by 10+ degrees.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of the faster, fainter halo shock feature seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated measurement of the leading edge based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement focusing on the southernmost portion of the CME as seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and its best fit with STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Single spacecraft analysis of the CME, taken originally as a plane-of-sky at E90, but considered a good approximation of the CME location to simulate. The source longitude could be E90-E100.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. LASCO C3 pylon obscures the front in running difference imagery. The CME front is dimpled and somewhat diffuse, so this analysis encompasses as much of the bright material as possible.

Single spacecraft analysis of the CME, taken originally as a plane-of-sky at E90, but considered a good approximation of the CME location to simulate. The source longitude could be E90-E100. CME front slightly less than 10 degrees half-width.

Very speculative, approximate analysis with two coronagraphs, initially based on the fitting of the clear halo front in STEREO A COR2A and later assuming that one of the less clear follow up fronts in SOHO/CCOR1 is the corresponding front (likely the front first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-03-07T00Z), however this front is more of a speculation. The speeds in analysis can vary depending on the chosen half width, which in this analysis was based on the SOHO LASCO imagery and set to 37 degrees.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2/GOES CCOR-1.
Updated measurement based on the approximate source location.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on the approximate source location.

Single spacecraft 3D analysis using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to no available STEREO A COR2 to triangulate, using the on-disk source longitude of E57. With possible deflection, the longitude could range E50-E65.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery as well as the source location in SWPC_CAT. Limited STEREO A images were available for this measurement due to the data gap.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery based on source location.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, using limited poor quality COR2 data tracking the faint front edge.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2, tracking the full width of the diffuse front as best as possible.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and limited and poor quality STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source longitude could vary E90-E110. The CME shape is quite dimpled, so the best fit here encompasses the full width of the material while the leading most edge is on the southern piece of the fit.
One-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO based) analysis of this very narrow jet, based on the possible source (high northern latitude eruption seen in STEREO A EUV 195 after 2026-03-14T10:45Z).

Single spacecraft 3D analysis using the observed latitude around S62 with some minor westward deflection, as the front is not resolvable in GOES CCOR-1 or STEREO COR2A for triangulation. Regardless, this CME is likely very far out of the ecliptic.

Single spacecraft 3D analysis using the observed latitude around S62 with some westward deflection, as the front is not resolvable in GOES CCOR-1 or STEREO COR2A for triangulation. Regardless, this CME is likely very far out of the ecliptic.
Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, a triangulated measurement was not available and the measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

Preliminary measurement following the brightest leading edge portions seen to the north and to the southeast. The measured parameters were based on the source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available and the measured parameters may vary as a result.
Measurement based on source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the potential source location. The CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and leading to additional uncertainty about the accuracy of the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.
Measurement based on the potential source location using STEREO A COR2 imagery only.

Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and faint frames of the front in SOHO LASCO C2. CME half width is less than 10 degrees, perhaps around 5 degrees, limited by the 10 degree half-width minimum in swpc_cat.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on source location near the NNE limb.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement relies heavily on later frames in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as there appears to be some acceleration as the CME propagates farther from the Sun.
Measurement based on probable source location on the SW limb around W90.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and on visible source location near NE limb.
Updated measurement based on STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 which have the clearest view of the separate features between CME: 2026-03-20T12:23Z and CME: 2026-03-20T12:38Z.
Preliminary analysis based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Very approximate, somewhat speculative analysis based on the fitting of the fuzzy faint front in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Very speculative analysis using triangulation and based on the fitting of two very faint fronts in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A coronagraphs.
3D analysis with one perspective, using SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1, as STEREO A COR2 does not observe the CME front.

Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery of the possible shock front of this CME. It is unclear if this is a shock or a fainter CME following the same path ahead of the brighter leading edge measurement.

Parameters based on best fit of brighter portion of CME as seen in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is some uncertainty with this CME as to whether this is a separate event or associated with the same eruption.
Final analysis of this CME with later imagery from SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 and with a few early timestamps of STEREO A COR2 before the routine evening data gap.

Analysis of the fainter wider shock front based on the fit with the imagery from SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs, including the early imagery from STEREO A COR2 before the usual evening data gap and fully available SOHO LASCO imagery.

Triangulated bulk analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. Due to the distorted lobed shape of the CME front, this analysis best captures the faster northern leading edge of the CME front using the full width of the bright bulk material. The CME front is further distorted in STEREO A COR2's field-of-view, but able to be captured good enough to track with the other coronagraphs.

SHOCK analysis using only SOHO LASCO C3, as the shock front is not resolvable in STEREO A COR2 imagery potentially due to faintness, being directed towards the spacecraft, and/or the COR2A data gap ending at 2026-03-23T03:38Z. The longitude could vary up to W55-W85, the source longitude of W65 is used as a best approximation with available imagery.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, using later frames only due to observed acceleration when including early frames. The CME shape is very distorted in STEREO A COR2 compared to SOHO LASCO, appearing much wider. Using STEREO A COR2's width, the leading edge still tracks very well between COR2 and C3.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Updated measurement with additional coronagraph imagery. Measurement based on the source location. The longitude is approximate based on location near the east limb.
Updated parameters based on SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery with matching to possible features in STEREO A COR2 following the data gap. Possible deflection within the FOV due to nearby CH.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, as the CME accelerates after LASCO C2 visibility. The CME front is notably wider in LASCO C3 than STEREO A COR2, so the LASCO C3 width is used.

Single spacecraft 3D analysis using W100 as an estimation of the source location of former AR 14392 which has rotated around the SW limb, as such the longitude could vary W95-W105. CME half width is barely 10 degrees, perhaps a little smaller.

Bulk analysis of the bright, inner material of this front observed best in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, which dissipates/blends into the background after its earliest frames. Triangulation puts it about 9 degrees west of the observed source, at E51 with observed E60, but the wide field line movement and EUV wave support this deflection.

Preliminary shock analysis, which may be considered final pending further review, using only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 as the shock front is not observed in STEREO A COR2 for triangulation. As such, the observed source region at E60 is used as a reference and the actual longitude may vary E50-E70. This is a wide analysis including a southern part of the feature believed to be of this same shock front and not a separate event, as the entire feature travels at the same speed through the coronagraphs.
Updated measurement of the leading edge with SOHO C2/C3 and STEREO A.
Leading edge of the jet with uncertainty in longitude approximately 10 degrees.
